title: Java Exceptions Try Catch
description: Practice try, catch, IOException, and finally.
learningGoals: Type a checked exception catch block.

completionChecks.prompt: Does the finally block run when readConfig throws IOException?
choices: No, catch prevents finally from running
Yes, finally runs after the catch block handles the exception
Only if the catch block rethrows the exception
Only if readConfig returns without throwing
completionChecks.explanation: finally always runs after the try/catch completes, including when a thrown exception was handled by catch, so the cleanup in finally is guaranteed.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if readConfig() throws a RuntimeException instead of IOException?
choices: The catch block still handles it because RuntimeException is a subtype of IOException
finally runs and then the RuntimeException propagates to the caller
finally is skipped because no catch clause matched
Compilation fails because RuntimeException is not declared by the try statement
completionChecks.explanation: catch (IOException e) only matches IOException and its subtypes; an unchecked RuntimeException bypasses it, but finally still runs before the exception continues up the call stack.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if readConfig throws an IOException and closeQuietly completes normally?
choices: The IOException message is printed, then the finally cleanup runs
The finally block runs first, then the catch block prints the message
The IOException skips both catch and finally
Compilation fails because IOException cannot be caught
completionChecks.explanation: A matching catch handles the IOException before control reaches finally, and finally still runs after the catch body.
